rows.Type: Frazer Nash Le Mans Replica / Le Mans Mk II
rows.Notes: Originally named "High Speed" and "Competition". 96-inch (2,438 mm) wheelbase. Cycle wings. Conventional (Bristol) gearbox.
Full width body.
Narrow body on Le Mans Replica chassis built to Formula 2 regulations. Also competed in World Championship Grand Prix and Formula Libre races.
Turismo (100 hp (75 kW)) or Gran Sport (125 hp (93 kW)) Bristol engine options. One car fitted with Austin Atlantic engine. Last 5 cars were open versions of Le Mans Coupé.
100 hp (75 kW) or 140 hp (100 kW) engine.
Full width body on Le Mans Replica Mk II chassis. 140 hp (100 kW) engine.

Essentially a sports version of the DKW Sonderklasse , which AFN also imported. As in that car, the engine is installed longitudinally and drives the front wheels. The car competed in the 1955 RAC Tourist Trophy without success, and was then laid up at the AFN works. It was brought back to life in 2008, and was featured in Classic & Sports Car , August 2011 issue.
V8 BMW engine. Listed at £3751 at the London Motor Show .
